data.title: Write failing unit tests for figma-api-fetch.sh (RED)
data.description:
## What
Write a failing unit test script tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh that specifies the observable behavior of figma-api-fetch.sh before implementation. Tests MUST FAIL because the script does not exist yet.

## testing_mode: RED
New source file, no prior coverage. Tests must be written and confirmed failing before implementation begins.

## Test Approach (Given/When/Then)
- FA-1: Given a valid file key and PAT, when figma-api-fetch.sh is called, then it invokes curl with Authorization: Bearer <pat> header and URL https://api.figma.com/v1/files/<key> and exits 0
- FA-2: Given a 401 response from Figma, when figma-api-fetch.sh processes it, then exits 1 with message on stderr about invalid PAT and re-provisioning instructions
- FA-3: Given a network timeout (curl exit 28), when figma-api-fetch.sh is called, then exits 1 with timeout error on stderr
- FA-4: Given PAT absent from config and FIGMA_PAT env var, when figma-api-fetch.sh runs, then reads PAT from FIGMA_PAT env var (env-var fallback)
- FA-5: Given a successful response, when figma-api-fetch.sh runs, then outputs raw Figma JSON to stdout (parseable by python3 json.loads)

## Files to Create
- tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh (executable bash test file)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh
- [ ] File contains test cases FA-1 through FA-5
  Verify: for t in FA-1 FA-2 FA-3 FA-4 FA-5; do grep -q "$t" tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh || exit 1; done
- [ ] Tests use a curl stub (stub replaces curl with a bash function or script in PATH) — no real HTTP calls
  Verify: grep -q 'stub\|mock\|fake\|PATH\|function curl' tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh
- [ ] Running tests exits non-zero (RED — script does not exist yet)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-figma-api-fetch.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0 (test gate does not block on this RED file)
